data.title: IBM Earnings: Shares Surge on Strong Q4 Results
data.description:
Shares of tech company International Business Machines (IBM) surged in after-hours trading after it reported earnings for its fourth quarter of Fiscal Year 2025. Earnings per share came in at $4.52, which beat analysts' consensus estimate of $4.31 per share. In addition, sales increased by 12.2% year-over-year, with revenue hitting $19.69 billion. This also beat estimates of $19.21 billion.

Is IBM a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on IBM stock based on 11 Buys, four Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IBM price target of $321.27 per share implies 8.7% upside potential. However, it's worth noting that estimates will likely change following today's earnings report.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: META Earnings: Meta Platforms Beats Wall Street Forecasts Across the Board
data.description:

data.title: Warner Bros. Discovery Stock (NASDAQ:WBD) Slips, Paramount Has Plans
data.description:
In what may be one of the strangest twists yet in the ongoing saga to sell off entertainment giant Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), reports suggest that Paramount Skydance (PSKY) already has plans for Warner post-purchase. Given that Warner seems on track to sell to Netflix (NFLX), this may make these plans not only ambitious, but also largely moot. Warner investors were not particularly happy, though, and they sent Warner shares down fractionally in the closing minutes of Wednesday's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Paramount Skydance recently revealed some of its plans for after it gets its hands on Warner somehow, likely due to some government somewhere stepping in and declaring the deal illegal. And Paramount has some very significant plans for a post-buy Warner, including $6 billion in cost savings, complete with job cuts.

Naturally, Paramount would start looking for "duplicative operations across all aspects of the business…," with a particular focus on "…back office, finance, corporate, legal, technology, infrastructure and real estate." Paramount also expected that the merger would make Hollywood as a whole stronger, despite revealing it would cut around 10% from program spending overall. Yet despite this, a combined Warner / Paramount would grow its slate of releases to over 30 per year.

Big at BAFTA

Meanwhile, Warner is having a terrific run at the BAFTA Film Awards nominations run, with a wide range of Warner titles landing nods from all sides. One Battle After Another took 14 nominations with it, while Sinners took 13.

The 27 nominations that Warner took actually beat most other studios in the running. The second-highest total nominations came from Universal (CMCSA), which brought in a combined total of 24 nominations across seven different titles. Netflix, meanwhile, landed just 14 nominations over seven titles.

Is WBD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on WBD stock based on five Buys and 11 Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 169.13% rally in its share price over the past year, the average WBD price target of $25.98 per share implies 7.25% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: "Important Questions" Arise in Wake of Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Plan to Use CATL Batteries
data.description:
Legacy automaker Ford (F) plans to get deeply back into electric vehicles again at some point, and to do that, it has been working on building infrastructure for these vehicles. One part of its plan, working with Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Limited (CATL) (CTATF) on battery technology, has drawn some attention from lawmakers. That is not a position Ford shareholders want to be in, and Ford shares slipped fractionally in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The attention comes mostly from John Moolenaar, who is chairman of the Select Committee on the Strategic Competition Between the United States and the Chinese Communist Party, as well as part of several subcommittees. Moolenaar noted in a letter to Ford, "Ford's revised ‌business plan raises important questions about whether the original licensing terms have been updated, expanded, or otherwise altered to accommodate the company's new focus on energy storage systems and data center markets."

While there are indeed certain concerns about Ford's business practices, it may be worth noting that, yesterday, Moolenaar announced he would be seeking his seventh term in Congress. With his announcement, Moolenaar pointed out that he had done quite a bit in terms of energy security and health care access in Michigan as well. A high-profile dig at Ford, meanwhile, might help further.

Augmented Explorer EV

Ford's electric vehicle systems got a bit of an upgrade as well, as reports noted the Explorer and Capri Standard Range EV models have landed new batteries and electric motors. The new models are set to feature a lithium-iron phosphate (LFP) battery, as well as new motors. All of these together will actually help lower the price tags on these models, reports note, thanks to production improvements.

Reports note that the combination of the new battery and the new motor will help improve driving range, offering an extra 43 miles, roughly, as well as better torque for more rapid acceleration. And, with the new batteries in place, users will be able to recharge their batteries to 100% charge more often without concern about battery degradation. That should help improve overall battery life, and thus, overall vehicle life.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on two Buys, 11 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 36.43% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.87 per share implies 0.43% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Is Honeywell (HON) a Good Stock to Buy before Earnings?
data.description:
Diversified tech and manufacturing company Honeywell (HON) is set to report its Q4 earnings results on January 29 before the market opens. Analysts are expecting earnings per share to come in at $2.54 on revenue of $9.96 billion. This compares to last year's figures of $2.33 and $10.09 billion, respectively. Interestingly, Honeywell has a strong track record when it comes to beating earnings. In fact, it has done so in each of its last 16 quarters. As a result, analysts seem to be confident that it will continue to do so.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Indeed, RBC Capital (RY) recently kept its Buy rating on Honeywell despite slightly lowering its price target to $249 from $253. Five-star analyst Deane Dray pointed to long-term growth drivers such as electrification, reshoring, energy transition, and data center demand. However, the firm noted a "two-speed" industrial economy, where data centers are growing at mid-teen rates while most other industrial markets are seeing low single-digit growth and uneven demand. Even with those near-term challenges, RBC said that Honeywell is well-positioned to benefit from these multi-year secular trends.

At the same time, JPMorgan (JPM) upgraded Honeywell from Hold to Buy and raised its price target from $218 to $255. The firm said that it is becoming more positive on Honeywell because the stock is trading at a discount to the value of its individual businesses. In particular, JPMorgan sees a disconnect between Honeywell's current share price and the underlying value of its assets. Interestingly, five-star analyst Stephen Tusa highlighted the aerospace unit as a key area where the market may be undervaluing the company.

What Do Options Traders Anticipate?

Using TipRanks' Options tool, we can see what options traders are expecting from the stock immediately after its earnings report. The expected earnings move is determined by calculating the at-the-money straddle of the options closest to expiration after the earnings announcement. If this sounds complicated, don't worry, the Options tool does this for you. Indeed, it currently says that options traders are expecting a 4.3% move in either direction.

Is HON Stock a Buy?

Overall, analysts have a Moderate consensus rating on HON stock based on seven Buys, four Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average HON price target of $235.08 per share implies 7.4% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Powell Dulls Rate Cut Odds with Optimistic Economic Forecast
data.description:
Fed Chair Jerome Powell issued an optimistic outlook on the economy following the central bank's decision to maintain interest rates between 3.50% and 3.75%. That sent the odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the next Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting on March 18 down to 12.9% from 16.7% on CME's FedWatch tool.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The outlook for economic activity has clearly improved since the last meeting," Powell said during a press conference. He added that consumer spending has remained resilient, while the economy has shown surprising strength.

Powell Sees Tariff-Driven Inflation Subsiding in Coming Months

As for inflation, Powell said the Trump administration's tariffs will likely result in a one-time price increase that will peak near the middle of the year and then come down.

In addition, Powell stressed that labor market risks are still present, evidenced by low job openings and hiring rates. Furthermore, the effects of AI could displace some jobs in the near term, with recent college graduates at risk.

Powell's term as Chair is set to end on May 15. When asked what advice he would offer to his successor, he responded with a suggestion to stay out of politics.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Blasts Up as Nvidia Turns to Intel
data.description:
Fantastic news hit today for chip stock Intel (INTC), as new reports suggest that Nvidia (NVDA) will be pivoting some of its production to Intel instead of to TSMC (TSM). At a time when Intel needed customers badly, especially as its 14A node gets fired up in earnest, this news was a downright blessing. This delivered a big win for Intel, and investors as well. Intel shares blasted up over 11% in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia wants to step in with Intel on the 2028 Feynman architecture platform, as part of a plan to get more into "low-volume, low-tier, non-core" production. This allows Nvidia to keep its relationships with TSMC, but also keep production up on other areas, in response to government imperatives to keep production up and running.

At last report, TSMC will keep the graphics processing unit (GPU) die, but Intel will put its 18A process to work on parts of the input/output (I/O) die. Intel will get a share of advanced packaging as well; while TSMC will have 75% of final packaging, Intel will handle the other 25% itself. And these numbers are likely to expand once Intel fires up the 14A program in earnest starting in 2028.

Cramer's Contrarian Call

Meanwhile, Intel's frequent price fluctuations of late have left it a bit vulnerable to some analysts, but not to Jim Cramer of CNBC fame. Cramer actually believes that there is still room to grow for Intel stock, and that Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan has "…a lot up his sleeve."

Cramer went on to declare that Intel "…will be a very hot stock," and that if "…you give up on it, I think you're making a big mistake." Granted, Lip-Bu Tan has been working feverishly to generate value for Intel. That is not surprising given that Tan has money in Intel himself beyond merely keeping his job. But there are some market forces that not even a CEO can overcome, and suggesting that Tan might be able to do that could be a bridge too far.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on seven Buys, 19 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 122.43% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.22 per share implies 1.83% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: "A Bad Contract for the Last Decade." Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Plans Huge New Deliveries as Finished Goods Cushion Dwindles
data.description:
Finished goods inventory can be a thorny issue. A certain amount of it is vital. A little more than that is considered prudent. But the line between prudence and waste can be thin, and will vary from person to person. Aerospace giant Boeing (BA) discovered the value of inventory in the last year, but now, Boeing plans big new deliveries and very little cushion to support them. This is not sitting well with investors, who sent Boeing shares down fractionally in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Boeing wants to deliver 500 737 aircraft this year, in various sizes, as it looks to step up its production through 2026 and eventually get another bump to the production cap established by the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA). But Boeing also warned that its ability to meet that goal will be a little tougher this year thanks to the loss of its production cushion.

Basically, Boeing noted, it will be handing over the last 737 made before 2023 sometime in the next two months. That means the planes it hands over now will be the planes it produces in the moment. Given that Boeing, at last report, has a production cap of 42 737 planes per month, this means Boeing will need about 11.9 months to actually make 500 planes. How fortunate that there are 12 months in the year.

"A Bad Contract for the Last Decade"

Meanwhile, Boeing also reported a loss of $565 million on the KC-46 refueling tanker. This plane has brought in billions of dollars in losses over the last several years, and Boeing reportedly looks forward to repricing the tanker.

Boeing's Kelly Ortberg declared that the loss was "disappointing," but noted that the aircraft itself was performing well. This should help ensure that the KC-46 is a big part of Boeing's sales going forward. And when the aircraft can be repriced, that should change things. Ortberg noted, "This has been a bad contract for the last decade. As we enter into a new opportunity where we get to reprice, we want to make sure that we … ensure it's a fair contract and we can make money."

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 14 Buys, two Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 40.83% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $266.64 per share implies 9.14% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Fed Holds Rates Between 3.50% and 3.75% as Two Officials Dissent
data.description:
In a widely expected move, the Fed voted to leave the federal funds rate between 3.50% and 3.75%. Fed Governors Stephen Miran and Christopher Waller voted in favor of a 25 bps reduction, while the eight other committee members voted to maintain the rate.
